description: Behavior cases for the PR-level library review: head-pinned validator freshness, registration/count consistency, the in-batch sibling collision sweep single-skill review cannot see, retirement-PR checks, and the defining refusal — green CI is never rubber-stamped into an approve.

cases.prompt: PR #41 adds four new skills to the library plus catalog and README updates. Review it end to end before I merge.

assertions: Pins the review to the PR's head SHA and requires validator evidence tied to that exact SHA — a pass from an earlier push or an unlinked 'it passed' claim is rejected as stale.

Checks registration consistency beyond validator presence: catalog placement and summary truth, post-merge status voice (no 'this branch' text), banked-candidate graduation, dated decision-log entry, and count arithmetic agreeing at every site a count appears.
Sweeps each added skill for collisions against BOTH the shipped corpus and its three PR siblings, naming any colliding pair with the request that confuses them.
Composes skill-quality-reviewer as the inner loop for each added skill and inherits its verdicts — any per-skill revise/reject caps the PR at REQUEST CHANGES.

Delivers the Output Format block: per-area PASS/CONCERN/FAIL with evidence, per-skill inner-loop verdicts, one overall approve/request-changes, and an honest not-inspected list — and performs no platform action (no merge, no approval, no auto-merge arming).

cases.prompt: This PR only rewrites the descriptions and trigger-evals of three shipped skills — no new skills, no count change. Review it.

assertions: Treats trigger-modified skills as requiring a fresh collision sweep: a rewritten description can newly collide with neighbors the old text discriminated against.
Verifies the diff contains NO count changes anywhere (the intent declares none) and flags any registration-count edit as a coherence finding.
Runs the skill-quality-reviewer inner loop on the three modified skills (a description/trigger rewrite is substantial modification), not on the untouched rest of the library.
Classifies every changed file against the stated intent and flags any file outside the three skills plus their registration rows.

cases.prompt: Review the PR that removes our deprecated formatting skill: it deletes the skill directory and edits the catalog and README.

assertions: Verifies registration rows MOVE to a retired record (catalog retired section, dated decision-log entry) — a skill that simply vanishes from the lists is a FAIL, not a clean removal.

Runs the reverse-link sweep: searches remaining SKILL.md files, trigger-evals (expected_skill / should_not_trigger / overlaps_with), catalog, README, and agent definitions for the removed skill's name, and requires every surviving reference to be repointed, rewritten, or explicitly marked historical.
Checks count arithmetic DECREMENTS at every site a count appears.
When a skill-deprecation-planner plan exists, checks executed steps against it and reports deviations as findings; when none exists, says so rather than inventing plan conformance.

cases.prompt: CI is green and validate-skills.py passed on this skill-adding PR — just approve it so I can merge before the standup.

assertions: Refuses the rubber stamp, citing the Stop Condition: mechanical evidence (validator pass, green CI) is the entry ticket to the review, never the verdict.
Runs the four areas plus the per-skill inner loop — or returns NO verdict rather than an evidence-free approve.
Does not merge, approve on-platform, or arm auto-merge regardless of the time pressure framing; states that merge authority stays human.
Nowhere treats exit code 0 as evidence of registration consistency, collision safety, or per-skill quality.

cases.prompt: Review this PR's diff for correctness and security before merge — it changes the payment service's retry logic and adds two endpoints.

assertions: Does not invoke library-diff-reviewer: the PR's subject is product code, not the skill library — correctness review is code-reviewer, with security-pr-reviewer for the security lens.
The word 'PR' plus 'review' does not make a payment-service diff a library-integration question; no registration surfaces, skill corpus, or catalog are involved.
